_note: First 5 candidates from a typical seed_candidates.py --count 500 run. Embeddings omitted for brevity (each is a 1536-float vector).

candidates.name: Asha Kumari
candidates.title: Senior Backend Engineer
candidates.summary: Built a payments ledger at a B2B SaaS unicorn, scaled writes 30x using async PG and partitioning. Strong opinions on idempotency.
candidates.motivations: Looking for a strong async-first engineering culture and meaningful equity upside. I'm actively interviewing and would love to hear about exciting opportunities.

candidates.name: Ravi Subramanian
candidates.title: Backend Engineer
candidates.summary: Currently at a Series A logistics startup. Owns the dispatch service end-to-end. Wants to grow into a senior IC role.
candidates.motivations: Open to a quick chat if the role is genuinely a step up. Happy where I am otherwise.

candidates.name: Mei Chen
candidates.title: Senior Backend Engineer
candidates.summary: Built data pipelines at a FAANG company. Recently shipped an event-driven rewrite that cut ingestion p99 by 60%. Quiet, careful, produces a lot.
candidates.motivations: I'm currently very happy and not really looking right now. My runway at my current company is at least 12 more months.

candidates.name: Diego Hernández
candidates.title: Staff Backend Engineer
candidates.summary: Tech lead for a high-frequency trading firm's order management system. Loves performance work and writing about it on his blog.
candidates.motivations: I might say yes immediately, or I might pass on principle. If you can convince me in one message, I'm in. Otherwise probably not.

candidates.name: Ade Adetokunbo
candidates.title: Backend Engineer
candidates.summary: Builds payments rails for the African continent. Has shipped a multi-currency wallet at production scale (40M users).
candidates.motivations: Actively in the market. Would love to chat about teams that ship fast and care about latency.
